posts.title: Service Overview — 인력소개 서비스 안내
posts.body:
## 인력소개 서비스 안내

BRIDGE의 서비스는 **채용 의뢰부터 강사 배치까지** 원스톱으로 진행됩니다.

### 서비스 절차

1. **채용 의뢰 접수**: 교육기관에서 원하는 강사 조건(경력, 국적, 시작일 등)을 알려주시면 됩니다
2. **후보자 매칭**: 조건에 맞는 후보자를 선별하여 이력서와 자기소개 영상을 전달합니다
3. **인터뷰 조율**: 교육기관과 후보자 간 화상 인터뷰를 주선합니다
4. **서류 검증**: 학위 공증, 범죄경력 조회서, TEFL 자격증 등 필수 서류를 확인합니다
5. **비자 지원**: 사증발급인정서(VIN) 신청 및 E-2 비자 발급 절차를 안내합니다
6. **입국 및 정착 지원**: 공항 픽업, 주거 안내, 외국인등록증 발급 등을 돕습니다

### 서비스 범위

| 항목 | 포함 여부 |
|------|----------|
| 후보자 모집 및 스크리닝 | O |
| 서류 검증 (학위, 범죄경력) | O |
| 인터뷰 조율 | O |
| 비자 컨설팅 | O |
| 근로계약서 검토 | O |
| 공항 픽업 | 지역별 상이 |
| 주거 알선 | 교육기관 협의 |

### 대상 교육기관

- **영어학원** (어학원, 보습학원)
- **유치원 / 어린이집** (영어 특별활동)
- **국제학교 / 외국인학교**
- **기업체 어학 프로그램**
- **공립학교** (EPIK/GEPIK 외 직접 채용)

채용 의뢰는 언제든 환영합니다. 현재 보유 후보자 현황은 문의 시 안내드립니다.

posts.title: First Time Hiring? — 원어민 채용 가이드
posts.body:
## 처음 원어민 강사를 채용하시나요?

원어민 강사 채용이 처음이신 원장님, 대표님을 위한 **기본 가이드**입니다.

### 채용 전 준비사항

**1. 사업장 요건 확인**
- 학원의 경우 **학원 설립·운영 등록증**이 필요합니다
- 외국인 고용 시 **고용허가**가 필요한지 관할 출입국에 확인하세요
- E-2 비자 초청을 위해서는 사업자등록증과 재무 관련 서류가 필요합니다

**2. 채용 조건 설정**
- 급여 범위 (2024년 기준 월 210~280만 원이 일반적)
- 주거 제공 여부 (원룸 제공 또는 주거수당)
- 근무 시간 및 수업 시수
- 계약 기간 (통상 1년)

**3. 필수 서류 이해**

원어민 강사가 한국에서 합법적으로 근무하려면 **E-2 비자**가 필요합니다. 이를 위해 강사가 준비해야 할 서류:

- 4년제 대학 학위증 (아포스티유 또는 영사 인증)
- 자국 범죄경력 조회서 (아포스티유 또는 영사 인증)
- 건강진단서 (한국 입국 후 발급 가능)
- TEFL/TESOL/CELTA 자격증 (120시간 이상 권장)

### 자주 묻는 질문

**Q. 비자 비용은 누가 부담하나요?**
A. 법적 의무 사항은 아니지만, 대부분의 교육기관이 비자 관련 비용을 부담합니다. 이는 경쟁력 있는 채용 조건의 일부입니다.

**Q. 채용까지 얼마나 걸리나요?**
A. 후보자 선정 후 서류 준비와 비자 발급까지 **약 4~8주**가 소요됩니다. 국내 거주 후보자의 경우 2주 이내도 가능합니다.

**Q. 에이전시 비용은 언제 발생하나요?**
A. BRIDGE는 **성공보수** 방식입니다. 채용이 확정되고 강사가 실제 근무를 시작한 후에 수수료가 발생합니다.

더 자세한 사항은 **문의하기**를 통해 상담받으세요.

posts.title: Registration Info — 직업소개소 등록 및 법적 안내
posts.body:
## 직업소개소 등록 및 법적 안내

BRIDGE는 관련 법률에 따라 정식 등록된 유료직업소개소입니다. 이 페이지에서는 직업소개 사업의 법적 근거와 이용자 보호 규정을 안내합니다.

### 법적 근거

**직업안정법** 제18조 및 동법 시행령에 따라, 유료직업소개사업을 영위하기 위해서는 관할 지방자치단체에 **등록**하여야 합니다.

- 등록 관청: 관할 시·군·구청
- 등록 요건: 사무실 확보, 보증보험 가입, 직업상담사 자격 등
- 갱신: 3년마다 등록 갱신 필요

### 수수료 규정

직업안정법 시행령에 따른 수수료 상한:

- **구직자(강사)**: 월 급여의 일정 비율 이내
- **구인자(교육기관)**: 별도 약정에 따름
- 수수료는 **채용 성사 후** 발생하며, 사전에 명확히 안내합니다

### 이용자 보호

- 개인정보는 **개인정보보호법**에 따라 엄격히 관리됩니다
- 수집된 개인정보는 채용 목적 외에 사용되지 않습니다
- 정보 주체의 요청 시 즉시 삭제 처리합니다 (법적 보존 의무 기간 제외)
- 모든 민감정보는 **AES-256 암호화**로 보호됩니다

### 분쟁 해결

서비스 이용 중 문제가 발생한 경우:

1. 먼저 BRIDGE에 직접 문의해 주세요
2. 해결이 어려운 경우 관할 **고용노동부 지방관서**에 민원을 제기할 수 있습니다
3. 한국소비자원을 통한 분쟁 조정도 가능합니다

투명하고 합법적인 서비스 운영을 약속드립니다.

posts.title: Pricing — 채용 서비스 요금 안내
posts.body:
## 채용 서비스 요금 안내

BRIDGE는 **성공보수(Success Fee)** 방식으로 운영됩니다. 채용이 성사되지 않으면 비용이 발생하지 않습니다.

### 요금 구조

**기본 원칙**
- 상담 및 후보자 추천: **무료**
- 수수료는 강사가 실제 **근무를 시작한 후** 청구됩니다
- 구체적인 수수료율은 채용 조건에 따라 달라지며, 계약 전 명확히 안내합니다

**포함 서비스**
- 후보자 모집, 스크리닝, 서류 검증
- 인터뷰 조율 및 통역 지원
- 비자 절차 컨설팅
- 근로계약서 검토 및 조언
- 입국 후 초기 정착 지원

### 보증 제도

BRIDGE를 통해 채용된 강사가 **계약 초기**에 퇴사하는 경우, 일정 조건 하에 **무료 재배치** 또는 **수수료 일부 환불**을 제공합니다.

| 퇴사 시점 | 보증 내용 |
|-----------|----------|
| 근무 시작 후 1개월 이내 | 무료 대체 인력 추천 또는 수수료 환불 |
| 1~3개월 이내 | 할인된 수수료로 대체 인력 추천 |
| 3개월 이후 | 보증 기간 종료 |

*보증 조건은 퇴사 사유에 따라 달라질 수 있습니다. 교육기관의 귀책 사유로 인한 퇴사는 보증 대상에서 제외됩니다.*

### 견적 요청

정확한 견적은 채용 조건(인원, 지역, 시작일, 경력 요건 등)에 따라 달라집니다. **문의하기** 페이지를 통해 상담을 요청해 주시면, 영업일 기준 1일 이내에 맞춤 견적을 보내드립니다.

모든 요금은 계약서에 명시되며, 숨겨진 비용은 없습니다.

posts.title: Our Team — BRIDGE 팀 소개
posts.body:
## Meet the BRIDGE Team
## BRIDGE 팀을 소개합니다

BRIDGE는 한국 교육 현장과 해외 인재 시장을 깊이 이해하는 전문가들로 구성되어 있습니다. 소규모이지만 효율적으로 운영되며, 모든 팀원이 교육기관과 강사 양쪽의 경험을 보유하고 있습니다.

### 팀 구성

**채용 매니저 (Recruitment Manager)**

교육기관의 채용 의뢰를 접수하고, 후보자 풀에서 최적의 매칭을 진행합니다. 학원, 유치원, 국제학교 등 다양한 교육기관의 채용 요구를 이해하고 있습니다.

- 교육기관 요구사항 분석
- 후보자 1차 스크리닝
- 인터뷰 조율 및 통역 지원

**비자 & 서류 전문가 (Visa & Documentation Specialist)**

E-2 비자 발급의 전 과정을 관리합니다. 7개 대상 국가별 서류 요건의 차이를 정확히 알고 있으며, 국가별 맞춤 가이드를 제공합니다.

- 국가별 서류 체크리스트 관리
- 아포스티유 / 영사 인증 절차 안내
- 사증발급인정서(VIN) 신청 지원

**강사 지원 코디네이터 (Teacher Support Coordinator)**

강사의 지원부터 입국, 정착, 그리고 근무 중 어려움까지 전반적인 서포트를 제공합니다.

- 이력서 작성 및 인터뷰 준비 코칭
- 입국 후 정착 지원 (은행 계좌, 외국인등록증 등)
- 근무 중 고충 상담 및 중재

### Our Approach / 우리의 접근 방식

우리는 단순히 사람을 배치하는 것이 아니라 **양쪽 모두가 만족하는 매칭**을 추구합니다.

- Every team member speaks both Korean and English fluently
- We have personally experienced the Korean ESL industry
- We maintain a small, focused team to ensure personalized service
- Each coordinator handles a limited number of cases at a time for quality over quantity

모든 팀원이 한국어와 영어에 능통하며, 한국 ESL 업계를 직접 경험한 실무자들입니다. 소수의 케이스에 집중하여 질 높은 서비스를 제공합니다.

### Contact

BRIDGE 팀에 대해 더 알고 싶으시면 **문의하기**를 통해 연락해 주세요. 어떤 질문이든 담당 코디네이터가 직접 답변드립니다.

posts.title: Partner Schools — 파트너 교육기관 안내
posts.body:
## Our Partner Schools
## 파트너 교육기관 안내

BRIDGE는 전국의 검증된 교육기관과 파트너십을 맺고 있습니다. 모든 파트너 학교는 **근무환경, 급여 지급 이력, 강사 만족도**를 기준으로 선별합니다.

### 파트너 기관 유형

**영어학원 (English Hagwons)**

가장 많은 채용이 이루어지는 기관 유형입니다. 유아부터 고등학생까지 다양한 연령대를 대상으로 합니다.

- 수업 시수: 주 25~30시간 (학원별 상이)
- 학생 수: 반당 6~15명
- 커리큘럼: 자체 교재 또는 출판사 교재 사용

English hagwons are the most common employer type. Students range from kindergarten to high school, with class sizes of 6-15 students.

**영어유치원 (English Kindergartens)**

만 3~7세 아이들을 대상으로 영어 몰입 환경을 제공하는 기관입니다. 오전 근무가 중심이며, 노래, 놀이, 미술 활동을 통한 영어 교육이 이루어집니다.

- 근무 시간: 보통 오전 9시 ~ 오후 3시 + 오후 학원 수업
- 특징: 활발하고 에너지 넘치는 근무 환경

English kindergartens provide immersive English environments for children ages 3-7. Morning-focused schedules with afternoon academy classes.

**국제학교 / 외국인학교 (International Schools)**

정규 교과 과정을 영어로 운영하는 학교입니다. 교사 자격증이나 석사 학위가 요구되는 경우가 많으며, 급여와 복리후생 수준이 높습니다.

- 요구 조건: 교사 자격증 또는 석사 학위 우대
- 급여: 학원 대비 상위 수준

**기업체 어학 프로그램 (Corporate Language Programs)**

기업 임직원 대상 비즈니스 영어 교육입니다. 성인 대상 경험이 있는 강사가 선호됩니다.

### 파트너 심사 기준

BRIDGE는 교육기관을 추천하기 전에 아래 사항을 확인합니다:

1. **급여 지급 이력**: 지연 없이 급여를 지급한 이력이 있는가
2. **숙소 상태**: 제공하는 숙소가 깨끗하고 안전한가
3. **근무 환경**: 초과근무 강요, 계약 위반 이력이 없는가
4. **기존 강사 피드백**: 이전에 배치된 강사의 만족도
5. **법적 요건 준수**: 4대보험 가입, 근로계약서 작성 등

We verify each partner school's salary payment history, housing conditions, working environment, teacher feedback, and legal compliance before recommending them to candidates.

### 현재 채용 중인 기관

최신 채용 공고는 **잡보드** 페이지에서 확인하실 수 있습니다. 공개되지 않은 비공개 포지션도 있으니 문의해 주세요.

To see current openings, visit our **Job Board**. Some positions are unlisted, so feel free to contact us for the full list of available positions.

posts.title: For Schools — 교육기관 채용 의뢰 방법
posts.body:
## How to Request a Teacher Through BRIDGE
## 교육기관 채용 의뢰 방법

원어민 강사 채용이 필요하신 교육기관을 위한 안내 페이지입니다.

### 의뢰 절차

**Step 1. 문의 접수**

웹사이트의 **문의하기** 또는 이메일을 통해 채용 의뢰를 접수합니다. 아래 정보를 함께 보내주시면 빠른 매칭이 가능합니다:

- 기관명 및 소재지
- 희망 시작일
- 학생 연령대 및 수업 과목
- 급여 및 숙소 제공 여부
- 선호 국적, 성별, 경력 (있을 경우)

Submit a recruitment request through our **Contact** page or email. Please include: school name, location, desired start date, student ages, salary, and housing details.

**Step 2. 조건 확인 및 공고 작성**

BRIDGE 담당자가 연락드려 채용 조건을 상세히 확인합니다. 확인된 내용을 바탕으로 영문 채용 공고를 작성합니다.

A BRIDGE coordinator will contact you to finalize job details and create an English-language job posting.

**Step 3. 후보자 추천**

조건에 맞는 후보자를 선별하여 이력서, 자기소개 영상, 자격증 사본을 전달합니다. 보통 **1~2주 내**에 첫 후보자를 추천합니다.

We screen and recommend qualified candidates, typically within 1-2 weeks.

**Step 4. 인터뷰 및 채용 확정**

화상 인터뷰를 주선하고, 필요 시 통역을 지원합니다. 채용 결정은 교육기관에서 직접 하십니다.

**Step 5. 서류 및 비자 진행**

채용이 확정되면 근로계약서 작성, 사증발급인정서 신청, 비자 발급까지 전 과정을 안내합니다.

**Step 6. 입국 및 배치**

강사 입국 후 외국인등록, 건강검진, 은행 계좌 개설 등 초기 정착을 지원합니다.

### 교육기관이 준비할 서류

| 서류 | 용도 |
|------|------|
| 사업자등록증 사본 | 사증발급인정서 신청 |
| 학원등록증 사본 | 교육기관 확인 |
| 납세증명서 | 재정 건전성 확인 |
| 근로계약서 (국/영문) | 비자 신청 첨부 |

### 비용

- 상담 및 후보자 추천: **무료**
- 수수료: 채용 성사 후 발생 (성공보수 방식)
- 수수료율은 채용 조건에 따라 상이하며, 계약 전 명확히 안내합니다

### 지원 범위

서울, 경기, 인천은 물론 **부산, 대구, 대전, 광주, 제주** 등 전국 어디서나 채용 의뢰가 가능합니다.

We support recruitment nationwide — Seoul, Gyeonggi, Incheon, Busan, Daegu, Daejeon, Gwangju, Jeju, and more.

채용 의뢰는 언제든 환영합니다. 최적의 강사를 찾아드리겠습니다.

posts.title: Our Values — BRIDGE가 추구하는 가치
posts.body:
## BRIDGE Values & Culture
## BRIDGE가 추구하는 가치

BRIDGE는 단순한 인력소개를 넘어, 한국 영어 교육 생태계의 건강한 발전에 기여하고자 합니다. 아래는 우리의 모든 업무를 관통하는 핵심 가치입니다.

### 1. Transparency / 투명성

채용 과정에서 숨기는 것이 없습니다. 급여, 근무 조건, 숙소 상태, 비자 절차 — 모든 정보를 사전에 명확히 공유합니다.

We believe in complete transparency. Salary, working conditions, housing quality, visa procedures — everything is communicated clearly before any commitment is made.

- 교육기관에게: 후보자의 경력, 자격, 강점과 약점을 솔직하게 전달합니다
- 강사에게: 학교의 실제 근무환경, 학생 수, 근무 시간을 정확하게 안내합니다

### 2. Quality Over Quantity / 양보다 질

대량 매칭보다 **정확한 매칭**을 추구합니다. 한 명의 코디네이터가 동시에 관리하는 케이스 수를 제한하여, 교육기관과 강사 모두에게 충분한 시간과 관심을 기울입니다.

We limit the number of active cases per coordinator to ensure personalized attention. One great match is worth more than ten mediocre ones.

### 3. Ethical Recruitment / 윤리적 채용

BRIDGE는 윤리적 채용 원칙을 철저히 준수합니다.

- **구직자에게 부당한 수수료를 청구하지 않습니다**
- 강제 근무, 여권 보관 등 비윤리적 관행을 묵인하지 않습니다
- 근로기준법을 위반하는 교육기관에는 후보자를 추천하지 않습니다
- 차별 없이 자격을 갖춘 모든 후보자에게 공정한 기회를 제공합니다

We do not charge unfair fees to job seekers. We do not tolerate passport confiscation, forced overtime, or any unethical labor practices. We do not recommend candidates to schools with a history of labor violations.

### 4. Long-Term Relationships / 장기적 관계

일회성 거래가 아닌 **지속적인 파트너십**을 추구합니다. 많은 교육기관이 해마다 BRIDGE를 다시 찾고, 많은 강사들이 계약 갱신 시에도 BRIDGE와 상담합니다.

- 매칭 후 최소 3개월간 사후 관리
- 문제 발생 시 중재 및 상담
- 계약 갱신 또는 이직 시 재상담 지원

### 5. Cultural Bridge / 문화적 가교

우리의 이름 그대로, BRIDGE는 한국과 해외 문화 사이의 **다리** 역할을 합니다.

Our name says it all. We bridge the gap between Korean educational institutions and international teachers — not just in language, but in expectations, work culture, and daily life.

- 한국 교육기관에게 외국인 강사의 문화적 배경과 기대를 설명합니다
- 외국인 강사에게 한국 직장 문화와 예절을 안내합니다
- 서로를 이해할 수 있는 기반을 만들어 성공적인 협업을 돕습니다

### Our Promise / 약속

BRIDGE를 통한 모든 채용 과정이 공정하고, 투명하며, 양쪽 모두에게 유익한 결과로 이어지도록 최선을 다하겠습니다.

Every recruitment process through BRIDGE will be fair, transparent, and beneficial for both parties. That is our commitment.

posts.title: FAQ — 자주 묻는 질문 모음
posts.body:
## Frequently Asked Questions
## 자주 묻는 질문

BRIDGE 서비스에 대해 가장 많이 문의하시는 질문을 모았습니다.

---

### For Teachers (강사용)

**Q. Do I need teaching experience to apply?**
No. While experience is preferred, many schools accept first-time teachers with a TEFL/TESOL certificate (120 hours or more). BRIDGE will match you with positions that fit your experience level.

경력이 없어도 지원할 수 있나요? 네. 경력이 있으면 유리하지만, TEFL/TESOL 자격증(120시간 이상)이 있으면 경력 없이도 지원 가능합니다.

**Q. Which nationalities are eligible for E-2 visas?**
Citizens of the following countries can qualify for an E-2 visa: USA, Canada, UK, Ireland, Australia, New Zealand, and South Africa. You must hold a bachelor's degree from an accredited university.

E-2 비자 대상 국적은 미국, 캐나다, 영국, 아일랜드, 호주, 뉴질랜드, 남아공입니다. 4년제 대학 학위가 필요합니다.

**Q. How long does the process take from application to arrival?**
For applicants outside Korea: typically 6-10 weeks from acceptance to arrival. For applicants already in Korea with a valid visa: as quick as 1-2 weeks.

지원부터 입국까지 해외 거주자는 보통 6~10주, 국내 거주자(유효한 비자 보유)는 1~2주가 소요됩니다.

**Q. Is housing provided?**
Most positions include furnished housing (single studio apartment) or a housing allowance. BRIDGE clearly indicates housing terms in each job listing.

대부분의 포지션에 원룸 형태의 숙소 제공 또는 주거수당이 포함됩니다.

**Q. Can I choose which city I work in?**
Yes. You can state your location preference, and BRIDGE will prioritize matching you with positions in that area. However, being flexible with location increases your options significantly.

네. 희망 지역을 말씀해 주시면 해당 지역 우선으로 매칭해 드립니다. 다만 지역에 유연할수록 선택지가 넓어집니다.

---

### For Schools (교육기관용)

**Q. 채용 의뢰 비용이 있나요?**
상담과 후보자 추천은 무료입니다. 수수료는 채용이 성사되고 강사가 근무를 시작한 후에만 발생합니다.

**Q. 후보자를 직접 선택할 수 있나요?**
물론입니다. BRIDGE는 조건에 맞는 후보자를 추천하지만, 최종 결정은 교육기관에서 인터뷰를 통해 직접 하십니다.

**Q. 강사가 조기 퇴사하면 어떻게 되나요?**
계약 초기(1개월 이내) 퇴사 시 무료 대체 인력을 추천합니다. 자세한 보증 조건은 서비스 계약서에 명시됩니다.

**Q. 비자 서류는 누가 준비하나요?**
강사가 해외에서 준비해야 할 서류(학위 공증, 범죄경력 조회 등)는 BRIDGE가 안내하고, 고용주가 한국에서 준비할 서류(사업자등록증, 사증발급인정서 신청 등)도 함께 안내드립니다.

---

기타 궁금한 사항은 **문의하기** 페이지를 통해 언제든 연락 주세요.

posts.title: About BRIDGE
posts.body:
## BRIDGE 소개

**BRIDGE**는 대한민국 전역의 교육기관과 우수한 원어민 영어 강사를 연결하는 전문 인력소개 에이전시입니다.

### 우리의 미션

한국 교육 현장에서 **양질의 원어민 강사 채용**이 점점 어려워지고 있습니다. BRIDGE는 이 문제를 해결하기 위해 설립되었습니다.

- **학원(어학원)**, **유치원**, **국제학교**, **기업체** 등 다양한 교육기관에 맞춤형 인재를 소개합니다
- 캐나다, 미국, 영국, 호주, 뉴질랜드, 남아공, 아일랜드 출신 강사를 모집합니다
- 서류 검증부터 비자 지원까지, 채용의 전 과정을 지원합니다

### 왜 BRIDGE인가?

1. **검증된 후보자 풀**: 학위 인증, 범죄경력 조회, TEFL/TESOL 자격증을 사전 확인합니다
2. **빠른 매칭**: 평균 2~3주 내에 적합한 후보자를 추천합니다
3. **비자 전문성**: E-2 비자 발급 및 이전 절차에 대한 풍부한 경험을 보유하고 있습니다
4. **사후 관리**: 채용 후에도 고용주와 강사 모두를 지속적으로 지원합니다

### 등록 정보

BRIDGE는 **직업안정법**에 따라 정식 등록된 **유료직업소개소**입니다. 관할 지방자치단체에 등록되어 있으며, 관련 법규를 철저히 준수합니다.

### 문의

채용 상담이나 서비스에 대한 문의는 웹사이트의 **문의하기** 페이지를 이용해 주세요. 영업일 기준 24시간 이내에 답변 드리겠습니다.

posts.title: Health Check Requirements for E-2 Visa Holders
posts.body:
## Health Check Requirements for Teaching in South Korea

All E-2 visa holders must complete a **medical examination** either before arrival or shortly after arriving in Korea. Here is what to expect and how to prepare.

### When Is the Health Check Required?

- **Before departure**: Some employers and consulates require a health check as part of the visa application.
- **After arrival**: Most teachers complete the health check within the **first two weeks** of arriving in Korea, as part of the ARC registration process.
- The specific timing depends on your **consulate** and **employer**.

### What the Health Check Includes

The standard medical examination for E-2 visa holders covers:

- **General physical examination** — Height, weight, blood pressure, vision, hearing
- **Chest X-ray** — Screening for tuberculosis (TB)
- **Blood tests** — Including HIV/AIDS screening and syphilis (VDRL)
- **Urinalysis** — Drug screening (marijuana, methamphetamines, opiates)
- **Mental health assessment** — Brief questionnaire

### Where to Get the Health Check in Korea

- Must be done at a **designated immigration medical institution** (usually a large hospital or public health center)
- Common locations:
  - **Seoul**: Severance Hospital, Samsung Medical Center, Seoul Medical Center
  - **Busan**: Busan Medical Center, Dong-A University Hospital
  - **Daegu**: Kyungpook National University Hospital
- Your employer will typically arrange and accompany you to the appointment.

### Cost

- Approximately **90,000 - 130,000 KRW** (varies by hospital)
- Some employers cover this cost; clarify in your contract negotiations.

### What to Bring

- **Passport**
- **2 passport photos** (3.5cm x 4.5cm)
- **Cash or card** for payment
- Wear **comfortable clothing** (you will need to change for the X-ray)

### Results Timeline

- Results typically take **3-5 business days**.
- Results are sent directly to Immigration or provided as a sealed document.

### Common Concerns

**Q: What if I fail the drug test?**
Drug use is taken **extremely seriously** in Korea. A positive result for illegal substances will result in visa denial, potential arrest, and deportation. Korea has **zero tolerance** for drugs, including marijuana, even if it is legal in your home country.

**Q: What if my chest X-ray shows something?**
If the X-ray shows anything concerning, you may need additional testing (sputum test). A history of treated TB is usually not disqualifying if you can prove completed treatment.

**Q: Can I use a health check from my home country?**
Generally **no** — Korea requires the examination to be conducted at a designated Korean medical institution. Some consulates accept home-country checks for the initial visa but will still require an in-Korea check upon arrival.

### Tips

- **Fast for 8 hours** before the appointment (water is OK) for accurate blood test results.
- Avoid alcohol for at least **48 hours** before the exam.
- If you take prescription medications, bring your **prescription documentation** in English.

posts.title: Alien Registration Card (ARC) Guide for ESL Teachers
posts.body:
## Alien Registration Card (ARC): Everything You Need to Know

The **Alien Registration Card (ARC)** is your official identification in South Korea. It is legally required for all foreigners staying longer than 90 days and is essential for everyday life in Korea.

### Why You Need an ARC

Your ARC is required for:

- **Opening a bank account**
- **Getting a phone plan**
- **Signing up for health insurance (NHIS)**
- **Internet and utility contracts**
- **Proof of legal residence** for any official purpose
- **Re-entry into Korea** (linked to your re-entry permit)

### When to Apply

You must apply for your ARC within **90 days of arrival** in Korea. Most teachers apply within the first 2-3 weeks, often alongside their health check.

### Required Documents

1. **Application Form** — Available at the Immigration Office or downloadable from HiKorea
2. **Passport** (original)
3. **Passport photo** — 3.5cm x 4.5cm, white background
4. **Employment contract** (copy)
5. **Business Registration Certificate** of your employer (copy)
6. **Health check results** (if completed)
7. **Fee**: 30,000 KRW

### Step-by-Step Process

1. **Book an appointment** on [HiKorea](https://www.hikorea.go.kr) — Walk-ins are possible but expect long waits.
2. **Visit your local Immigration Office** — Go to the office that covers your residential area.
3. **Submit documents and fingerprints** — You will provide fingerprints and a digital photo at the office.
4. **Pay the fee** — 30,000 KRW (cash, card, or revenue stamp).
5. **Receive your ARC** — Cards are mailed to your registered address within **2-3 weeks**. You will receive a text notification when it ships.

### Your ARC Number

Your ARC number follows the format: **000000-0000000** (similar to a Korean resident registration number). This number is used for:

- Tax purposes
- Health insurance enrollment
- Banking and financial services
- Online age/identity verification on Korean websites

### Important Responsibilities

- **Address changes**: If you move, you must report your new address to Immigration within **14 days**. This can be done at the local district office (gu-cheong) or on HiKorea.
- **Lost ARC**: Report immediately to Immigration and apply for a replacement (50,000 KRW).
- **Return on departure**: When you **permanently leave Korea**, you must return your ARC at the airport immigration counter.

### Tips

- **Take a photo** of both sides of your ARC and store it securely in the cloud. This is invaluable if you lose it.
- Your employer should help you with this process, including providing necessary company documents.
- The **1345 Immigration Helpline** is available in English and can answer specific questions about your case.
- Some immigration offices are less crowded than others. If you live near a major city, consider visiting a **suburban branch** for shorter wait times.

posts.title: Re-entry Permit Information for E-2 Visa Holders
posts.body:
## Re-entry Permits: What ESL Teachers Need to Know

If you plan to leave South Korea temporarily during your teaching contract (vacation, family visit, etc.), you need to understand the **re-entry permit** system to avoid losing your visa status.

### Current Policy (as of 2024)

South Korea currently operates under a system where **most ARC holders receive automatic re-entry permission**. However, the policy has changed multiple times, so always verify before traveling.

### Types of Re-entry Permits

| Type | Description | Validity |
|------|-------------|----------|
| **Single** | One departure and return | Valid for up to 1 year |
| **Multiple** | Unlimited departures | Valid for duration of visa |

### Who Needs a Separate Re-entry Permit?

- As of recent policy, E-2 visa holders with a valid ARC generally have **automatic re-entry permission** and do not need to apply separately.
- However, this policy can be **suspended or changed** at any time (as happened during COVID-19).
- Always check the **latest policy** on [HiKorea](https://www.hikorea.go.kr) or by calling **1345** before booking travel.

### How to Apply (If Required)

1. **Online**: Apply through HiKorea portal
2. **In person**: Visit your local Immigration Office
3. **At the airport**: Available at some airports (Incheon, Gimpo) but **not recommended** as a primary plan due to long lines and limited hours

### Cost (If Required)

- **Single re-entry**: 30,000 KRW
- **Multiple re-entry**: 50,000 KRW

### What Happens If You Leave Without One?

If a re-entry permit is required and you leave Korea without one:

- Your **ARC becomes invalid**
- Your **visa status is forfeited**
- You will need to **apply for a new visa** from scratch at a Korean consulate in your home country
- This means new background check, new apostille, new everything

### Travel Tips for E-2 Holders

- **Always carry your ARC** when traveling internationally, in addition to your passport.
- **Notify your employer** before traveling, as some contracts have specific clauses about vacation travel.
- **Check your visa expiry date** — do not travel if your visa will expire while you are abroad.
- **Keep your return flight booking** accessible, as immigration may ask to see it upon re-entry.
- **Airport Immigration**: Use the **foreigner lines** at passport control. Some airports have **automated gates** that ARC holders can register for, significantly reducing wait times.

### Registering for Automated Immigration Gates

E-2 holders can register for **Smart Entry Service (SES)** at:

- Incheon Airport Immigration Office
- Any regional Immigration Office

Requirements: Passport + ARC + fingerprint registration. Once set up, you can use the automated gates for faster departure and arrival processing.

posts.title: Hi Korea Portal — 하이코리아 출입국 온라인 민원
posts.body:
## Hi Korea — Immigration Online Portal
**하이코리아 출입국 온라인 민원**

**Hi Korea** (www.hikorea.go.kr) is the official online portal of the Korea Immigration Service. Teachers and employers can use it for various immigration tasks without visiting an office in person.

### What You Can Do on Hi Korea

- **Check visa status** and remaining period of stay
- **Apply for a Visa Issuance Number (VIN)** — employers submit the application online
- **Book appointments** at immigration offices
- **Apply for visa extensions** (some visa types)
- **Report changes** — address, workplace, passport info
- **Check application status** and processing progress

### How to Register

1. Visit **hikorea.go.kr** and click "Sign Up"
2. Choose **Foreigner** or **Korean Employer** registration
3. For foreigners: You will need your **Alien Registration Number** or passport number
4. For employers: You will need your **Business Registration Number**
5. Complete identity verification
6. Create your account

### Tips for Using Hi Korea

- The site works best on **Internet Explorer / Edge** with Korean security plug-ins installed (ActiveX may be required for some features)
- **Chrome** compatibility has improved but some features may still require Edge
- Upload documents in **PDF or JPG** format, under 5MB each
- The system may be slow during peak hours (Monday mornings, after holidays)
- Keep your **application receipt number** (접수번호) — you'll need it to track progress

### Booking an Immigration Office Visit

Many immigration offices now require **advance reservations**:

1. Log in to Hi Korea
2. Go to "Reservation" (방문예약)
3. Select your regional immigration office
4. Choose the service type and preferred date/time
5. Print or screenshot your reservation confirmation

### Important Immigration Office Contacts

- **Seoul Immigration Office**: 02-2650-6212
- **Suwon Immigration Office**: 031-695-4000
- **Immigration Contact Center (1345)**: Multilingual support available in English, Chinese, Vietnamese, and more

posts.title: Leaving Korea — 출국 준비 체크리스트 (비 F비자)
posts.body:
## Leaving Korea Checklist
**출국 준비 체크리스트 (비 F비자)**

Whether your contract has ended or you are moving on to new adventures, here is everything you need to do before leaving Korea on an E-2 or other non-F class visa.

### 4 Weeks Before Departure

- [ ] **Notify your employer** in writing about your departure date
- [ ] **Check your contract** for any end-of-contract obligations (notice period, severance, flight reimbursement)
- [ ] **Pension refund**: Visit the National Pension Service (NPS) office or apply online. Citizens of countries **without** a pension agreement with Korea can receive a **lump-sum refund** at the airport or after departure
- [ ] **Cancel recurring payments**: gym memberships, subscriptions, phone plan

### 2 Weeks Before Departure

- [ ] **Close your bank account** or set up international transfer for remaining balance
- [ ] **Return your ARC**: You must surrender your Alien Registration Card at the airport upon departure
- [ ] **Cancel health insurance**: Notify NHIS if you were enrolled
- [ ] **Settle utility bills**: gas, electricity, water, internet
- [ ] **Clean your apartment**: Take photos for documentation before handing back keys
- [ ] **Get a work experience certificate** (경력증명서) from your employer

### At the Airport

- [ ] **Return ARC** at the immigration desk (there is a dedicated drop-off box at Incheon Airport)
- [ ] **Pension refund kiosk** at Incheon Airport (available for eligible nationalities)
- [ ] **Tax refund**: If you have any tax-free shopping receipts, process them before checking in

### After Leaving Korea

- **Pension refund** (if not processed at airport): Apply through the NPS website with your Korean bank account details or international bank info
- **Severance pay**: Should be deposited within 14 days of your last working day
- **Tax settlement**: Your employer should have completed your year-end tax settlement; request documentation if needed

### Country-Specific Pension Notes

| Country | Lump-sum Refund? |
|---------|------------------|
| USA | Yes (subject to 19.4% tax) |
| Canada | Yes |
| UK | Yes |
| Australia | Agreement — no lump-sum |
| Ireland | Yes |

posts.title: New Zealand — E-2 Visa Documents 서류 안내
posts.body:
## New Zealand — E-2 Visa Document Guide
**서류 안내 (뉴질랜드)**

New Zealand citizens are eligible for E-2 visas in South Korea. Here is a step-by-step guide to preparing your documents.

### Criminal Background Check (NZ Ministry of Justice)

1. Apply through the **NZ Ministry of Justice** website: **justice.govt.nz**
2. Select **"Criminal record request for overseas use"**
3. Provide your full legal name, date of birth, and any previous names
4. Pay the fee: **NZD $30** for standard, **NZD $60** for urgent
5. Standard processing: **10-20 working days**
6. Urgent processing: **3-5 working days**
7. The result will be mailed to your NZ address

### Degree Authentication

New Zealand is a Hague Convention member, so the Apostille process applies:

1. **Get a certified copy** of your degree from your university or a NZ Notary Public
2. Apply for an **Apostille** from the **Department of Internal Affairs (DIA)**

### Apostille Process (DIA)

1. Visit **dia.govt.nz** and search for "Apostille"
2. Complete the application form
3. Submit your notarized/certified document to DIA
4. Fee: **NZD $44** per document
5. Processing: **10-15 working days** by mail
6. In-person service available in Wellington for faster processing

### Complete Document Checklist

- [ ] **Passport** — valid 6+ months
- [ ] **Bachelor's degree** — certified and apostilled
- [ ] **Criminal background check** — from Ministry of Justice, apostilled
- [ ] **Passport photos** — 3.5cm x 4.5cm, white background
- [ ] **TEFL/TESOL certificate** — 120+ hours (recommended)
- [ ] **VIN letter** — from your Korean employer
- [ ] **Signed employment contract**

### Korean Embassy / Consulate in NZ

- **Korean Embassy in Wellington**: Level 11, ASB Bank Tower, 2 Hunter Street
- Phone: +64 4 473 9073
- Visa applications accepted Monday-Friday, 9:00 AM - 12:00 PM

### Tips

- NZ criminal checks can take **up to 20 days** — apply as early as possible
- If you have lived in another country for 12+ months, you may need a background check from that country as well
- BRIDGE can guide you through the entire process — reach out for assistance

posts.title: Seoul 서울 — 수도권, 한류의 중심지
posts.body:
## Seoul — The Heart of South Korea
## 서울 — 수도권, 한류의 중심지

Seoul is South Korea's capital and the cultural, economic, and educational hub of the country. With a population of nearly 10 million (25 million in the greater metro area), it is one of Asia's most dynamic cities.

서울은 대한민국의 수도이자 문화, 경제, 교육의 중심지입니다. 인구 약 1,000만 명(수도권 2,500만 명)으로 아시아에서 가장 역동적인 도시 중 하나입니다.

### Teaching in Seoul

Seoul has the **highest concentration of English hagwons** in the country. Competition for positions is strong, but so are the opportunities.

서울에는 전국에서 가장 많은 영어학원이 밀집해 있습니다. 경쟁이 치열하지만 기회도 풍부합니다.

- **Salary range**: 2,200,000 - 2,800,000 KRW/month (higher for experienced teachers)
- **급여 범위**: 월 220만 ~ 280만 원 (경력에 따라 상이)
- **Popular teaching areas**: Gangnam, Seocho, Songpa, Mapo, Nowon
- **주요 교육 지역**: 강남, 서초, 송파, 마포, 노원

### Living in Seoul

**Transportation**: Seoul's subway system has 23 lines and covers virtually the entire city. A single ride costs 1,400 KRW. Buses are equally extensive and efficient.

**교통**: 서울 지하철은 23개 노선으로 도시 전역을 커버합니다. 기본 요금 1,400원. 버스 네트워크도 촘촘합니다.

**Cost of Living**:
- Dining out: 8,000-15,000 KRW per meal
- Coffee: 4,000-6,000 KRW
- Groceries: 300,000-500,000 KRW/month
- Entertainment: Endless options from Hongdae clubs to Bukchon hanok village

### Neighborhoods for Teachers

| Area | Vibe | Foreigner-Friendly |
|------|------|--------------------|
| **Itaewon/Haebangchon** | International, diverse dining | Very high |
| **Hongdae** | Young, artistic, nightlife | High |
| **Gangnam** | Upscale, business district | Moderate |
| **Jamsil** | Family-friendly, near Lotte World | Moderate |
| **Mapo/Hapjeong** | Trendy cafes, Han River access | High |

### Why Choose Seoul?

- Unmatched convenience and infrastructure
- Vibrant international community
- Endless cultural experiences: palaces, museums, K-pop, street food
- Best healthcare facilities in the country
- Direct flights to major global destinations

서울에서의 생활은 편리함과 다양한 문화 경험을 동시에 제공합니다. BRIDGE가 서울 지역 최적의 포지션을 찾아드립니다.

posts.title: Gyeonggi-do 경기도 — 서울 근교 최대 교육 밀집 지역
posts.body:
## Gyeonggi-do — Seoul's Surrounding Province
## 경기도 — 서울 근교 최대 교육 밀집 지역

Gyeonggi Province surrounds Seoul and is home to over 13 million people, making it the most populous province in South Korea. It offers excellent teaching opportunities with a slightly lower cost of living than Seoul.

경기도는 서울을 둘러싸고 있으며 인구 1,300만 명 이상으로 대한민국에서 가장 인구가 많은 광역자치단체입니다. 서울보다 약간 낮은 생활비로 훌륭한 교육 기회를 제공합니다.

### Key Cities for ESL Teachers

**Suwon (수원)** — Provincial capital, Samsung headquarters
- Population: ~1.2 million
- 30-50 min from Seoul by subway
- Suwon Hwaseong Fortress (UNESCO World Heritage)

**Yongin (용인)** — Everland, Korean Folk Village
- Popular with families, many hagwons
- Growing international community

**Seongnam/Bundang (성남/분당)** — Korea's tech hub
- Modern new city, excellent infrastructure
- Higher salaries comparable to Seoul
- Home to many IT companies (Naver, Kakao offices nearby)

**Goyang/Ilsan (고양/일산)** — Northwest of Seoul
- Lakeside living near Ilsan Lake Park
- Quick access to Seoul via subway Line 3

### 경기도에서 가르치기

- **Salary range**: 2,100,000 - 2,600,000 KRW/month
- **급여 범위**: 월 210만 ~ 260만 원
- Housing is often **more spacious** than Seoul apartments
- 주거 공간이 서울보다 넓은 경우가 많습니다
- Many schools provide **newer facilities** than older Seoul academies

### Living in Gyeonggi-do

**Pros**:
- Lower rent and living costs (10-20% less than Seoul)
- More space — larger apartments, less crowded
- Easy Seoul access via subway, bus, or KTX
- Nature: mountains, parks, and riverside trails
- Family-friendly environment

**Cons**:
- Less nightlife compared to Seoul
- Fewer international restaurants and bars
- Some areas may feel suburban

### Transportation

Gyeonggi-do is well-connected to Seoul through:
- **Seoul Metro extensions** (Lines 1, 3, 4, Bundang Line, Shinbundang Line)
- **GTX** (Great Train Express) — new express rail connecting Gyeonggi cities to Seoul in under 30 minutes
- **Intercity buses** — frequent and affordable

경기도는 서울의 편리함을 누리면서도 더 여유로운 생활을 원하는 강사에게 이상적인 지역입니다.

posts.title: Incheon 인천 — 국제공항 소재, 서울 서쪽 관문
posts.body:
## Incheon — Gateway to Korea
## 인천 — 국제공항 소재, 서울 서쪽 관문

Incheon is South Korea's third-largest city with a population of about 3 million. Home to Incheon International Airport, it serves as Korea's primary gateway to the world.

인천은 인구 약 300만 명의 대한민국 제3의 도시입니다. 인천국제공항이 위치해 있어 한국의 관문 역할을 합니다.

### Teaching in Incheon

Incheon offers a good balance of urban living and coastal charm. The city has a growing demand for English teachers, particularly in newer residential areas.

인천은 도시 생활과 해안 도시의 매력을 동시에 제공합니다. 특히 신도시 지역에서 영어 강사 수요가 꾸준히 증가하고 있습니다.

- **Salary range**: 2,100,000 - 2,500,000 KRW/month
- **급여 범위**: 월 210만 ~ 250만 원
- **Popular areas**: Songdo, Bupyeong, Juan, Yeonsu
- **주요 지역**: 송도, 부평, 주안, 연수

### Songdo International City (송도국제도시)

Songdo deserves special mention as one of Korea's most modern planned cities:

- Built from scratch on reclaimed land
- Home to international organizations and universities
- Cutting-edge architecture and green spaces (Central Park)
- Many international families — strong demand for quality English education
- Higher salaries than the Incheon average

### Living in Incheon

**Cost of Living** (compared to Seoul):
- Rent: 20-30% lower
- Dining: 10-15% lower
- Groceries: Similar prices
- Transportation to Seoul: 1,400 KRW by subway (40-60 min to central Seoul)

**Highlights**:
- **Chinatown** (차이나타운) — Korea's only official Chinatown, great food
- **Wolmido** (월미도) — Island with amusement park and seafood restaurants
- **Coastal scenery** — Beautiful islands and beaches accessible by ferry
- **Incheon Airport proximity** — Perfect for weekend trips abroad

### Transportation

- **Seoul Metro Line 1** runs directly from Incheon to Seoul Station
- **Incheon Metro** Lines 1 and 2 cover the city
- **Airport Railroad (AREX)** connects to Seoul Station in 43 minutes
- **Buses** to Seoul are frequent (every 10-15 min during peak hours)

### Why Choose Incheon?

- Modern living at lower costs than Seoul
- Coastal lifestyle with ocean views and fresh seafood
- Easy access to Seoul for weekend activities
- International atmosphere, especially in Songdo
- Airport convenience for travel

BRIDGE는 인천 지역의 검증된 교육기관을 소개해 드립니다.

posts.title: Busan 부산 — 해양 도시, 대한민국 제2도시
posts.body:
## Busan — Korea's Coastal Metropolis
## 부산 — 해양 도시, 대한민국 제2도시

Busan is South Korea's second-largest city with a population of 3.4 million. Known for its stunning beaches, vibrant seafood markets, and laid-back atmosphere, it offers a distinctly different experience from Seoul.

부산은 인구 340만 명의 대한민국 제2의 도시입니다. 아름다운 해변, 활기찬 해산물 시장, 여유로운 분위기로 서울과는 확연히 다른 경험을 제공합니다.

### Teaching in Busan

Busan has a robust ESL market with many hagwons concentrated in the Seomyeon and Haeundae areas.

부산은 서면과 해운대 지역을 중심으로 활발한 ESL 시장을 형성하고 있습니다.

- **Salary range**: 2,100,000 - 2,500,000 KRW/month
- **급여 범위**: 월 210만 ~ 250만 원
- **Key teaching areas**: Seomyeon, Haeundae, Nampo-dong, Dongnae
- **주요 교육 지역**: 서면, 해운대, 남포동, 동래

### Living in Busan

**The Beach Life**: Unlike any other major Korean city, Busan offers genuine beach living. Haeundae Beach, Gwangalli Beach, and Songjeong Beach are all accessible by subway.

**해변 생활**: 부산은 한국의 대도시 중 유일하게 진정한 해변 생활을 즐길 수 있는 곳입니다.

**Cost of Living** (compared to Seoul):
- Rent: 25-35% lower
- Dining: 15-20% lower (especially seafood)
- Entertainment: Comparable
- Overall: Noticeably more affordable

### Must-Experience in Busan

- **Jagalchi Fish Market** (자갈치시장) — Korea's largest seafood market
- **Gamcheon Culture Village** (감천문화마을) — Colorful hillside art village
- **Haedong Yonggungsa Temple** (해동용궁사) — Stunning seaside temple
- **BIFF Square** (부산국제영화제 광장) — Street food heaven
- **Taejongdae** (태종대) — Coastal cliffs with panoramic ocean views

### Busan's Foreigner Community

Busan has an active expat community centered around:

- **Haeundae** — Beach bars, international restaurants
- **Kyungsung/PNU area** — University district, younger crowd
- **Gwangalli** — Craft beer scene, bridge views

### Transportation

- **Busan Metro**: 6 lines covering major areas
- **KTX to Seoul**: 2 hours 30 minutes
- **Gimhae International Airport**: Direct flights to Japan, Southeast Asia, and more

부산은 활기찬 도시 생활과 여유로운 해변 문화를 동시에 즐기고 싶은 강사에게 최적의 선택입니다.

posts.title: Daegu 대구 — 내륙 거점 도시
posts.body:
## Daegu — Korea's Inland Hub
## 대구 — 내륙 거점 도시

Daegu is South Korea's fourth-largest city with a population of about 2.4 million. Located in the southeastern inland region, it is known for its warm climate, affordable living, and strong educational culture.

대구는 인구 약 240만 명의 대한민국 제4의 도시입니다. 동남부 내륙에 위치하며, 따뜻한 기후, 저렴한 생활비, 그리고 강한 교육열로 유명합니다.

### Teaching in Daegu

Daegu has a healthy ESL market with consistent demand for native English teachers. The city is particularly known for its strong hakwon (private academy) culture.

대구는 원어민 영어 강사에 대한 꾸준한 수요가 있는 건강한 ESL 시장을 보유하고 있습니다. 특히 학원 문화가 강한 도시로 알려져 있습니다.

- **Salary range**: 2,100,000 - 2,400,000 KRW/month
- **급여 범위**: 월 210만 ~ 240만 원
- **Key areas**: Suseong-gu (수성구) — Daegu's education district, Junggu, Dalseong
- **주요 지역**: 수성구 (대구의 교육 중심지), 중구, 달성

### Why Daegu?

**Affordability**: Daegu is one of the most affordable major cities in Korea.

**합리적인 생활비**: 대구는 한국 대도시 중 가장 생활비가 저렴한 도시 중 하나입니다.

| Item | Daegu | Seoul |
|------|-------|-------|
| Studio rent | 300,000-450,000 KRW | 500,000-900,000 KRW |
| Meal (restaurant) | 7,000-10,000 KRW | 9,000-15,000 KRW |
| Coffee | 3,500-4,500 KRW | 4,500-6,000 KRW |

### Living in Daegu

**Climate**: Daegu is nicknamed "Daefrica" (대프리카) for its hot summers — temperatures can reach 38°C+. Winters are milder than Seoul.

**기후**: 대구는 여름 폭염으로 유명합니다 (최고 38°C 이상). 겨울은 서울보다 온화합니다.

**Highlights**:
- **Seomun Market** (서문시장) — One of Korea's largest traditional markets
- **Apsan Park** (앞산공원) — Cable car with panoramic city views
- **Dongseongno** (동성로) — Downtown shopping and dining street
- **Palgongsan Mountain** (팔공산) — Popular hiking destination
- **Suseong Lake** (수성못) — Beautiful lakeside walking area

### Transportation

- **Daegu Metro**: 3 lines covering the city center
- **KTX to Seoul**: 1 hour 40 minutes
- **KTX to Busan**: 45 minutes
- **Daegu International Airport**: Limited but growing international routes

### Expat Community

Daegu's foreigner community is smaller but tight-knit. Social events, language exchange meetups, and hiking groups are common. The downtown Dongseongno area has several foreigner-friendly bars and restaurants.

대구의 외국인 커뮤니티는 규모는 작지만 결속력이 강합니다.

대구는 저렴한 생활비로 저축을 극대화하면서 한국 생활을 깊이 경험하고 싶은 강사에게 추천합니다.

posts.title: Daejeon 대전 — 과학 수도, 대한민국 중부 거점
posts.body:
## Daejeon — Korea's Science Capital
## 대전 — 과학 수도, 대한민국 중부 거점

Daejeon is South Korea's fifth-largest city with a population of about 1.5 million. Located in the center of the country, it serves as a major transportation hub and is home to Korea's leading science and research institutions.

대전은 인구 약 150만 명의 대한민국 제5의 도시입니다. 국토 중앙에 위치하여 교통의 요충지이며, 대한민국을 대표하는 과학 및 연구 기관이 집중된 도시입니다.

### Teaching in Daejeon

Daejeon has a steady demand for English teachers, driven by its large student population and the families of researchers and professionals working in the Daedeok Science Complex.

대전은 대덕연구단지의 연구원 가족과 많은 학생 인구 덕분에 영어 강사에 대한 꾸준한 수요가 있습니다.

- **Salary range**: 2,100,000 - 2,400,000 KRW/month
- **급여 범위**: 월 210만 ~ 240만 원
- **Key teaching areas**: Yuseong-gu (유성구) — university/research district, Seo-gu, Jung-gu
- **주요 교육 지역**: 유성구 (대학/연구 중심지), 서구, 중구

### Why Daejeon?

**Central Location**: Daejeon is often called the "crossroads of Korea." KTX takes you to Seoul in just 50 minutes and to Busan in 1 hour 40 minutes. Virtually anywhere in the country is within easy reach.

**중심 입지**: 대전은 "한국의 교차로"로 불립니다. KTX로 서울까지 50분, 부산까지 1시간 40분. 전국 어디든 쉽게 갈 수 있습니다.

**Affordability**: Daejeon offers metropolitan convenience at significantly lower costs than Seoul.

| Item | Daejeon | Seoul |
|------|---------|-------|
| Studio rent | 250,000-400,000 KRW | 500,000-900,000 KRW |
| Meal (restaurant) | 7,000-9,000 KRW | 9,000-15,000 KRW |
| Coffee | 3,500-4,500 KRW | 4,500-6,000 KRW |

### Living in Daejeon

**Yuseong District (유성구)**: The most popular area for foreigners. Home to KAIST (Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology), Chungnam National University, and the Daedeok Science Complex. Hot spring spas are a local specialty.

**유성구**: 외국인이 가장 많이 거주하는 지역. KAIST, 충남대학교, 대덕연구단지가 위치. 유성온천이 유명합니다.

**Highlights**:
- **Expo Science Park** (엑스포과학공원) — Built for the 1993 Expo, now a family-friendly park
- **Gyejoksan Mountain** (계족산) — Famous barefoot walking trails
- **Hanbat Arboretum** (한밭수목원) — One of Korea's largest urban arboretums
- **Seongsimdang Bakery** (성심당) — Legendary bakery, a Daejeon institution since 1956
- **Daecheong Lake** (대청호) — Scenic reservoir for cycling and hiking

### Transportation

- **Daejeon Metro**: Line 1 covering the city center (east-west)
- **KTX to Seoul**: 50 minutes
- **KTX to Busan**: 1 hour 40 minutes
- **Express Bus Terminal**: Connections to all major cities
- **Daejeon is the central KTX hub** — any direction in Korea is accessible quickly

### Expat Community

Daejeon's international community is centered around KAIST and the research institutes in Yuseong. There are regular language exchange events, international food meetups, and weekend hiking groups. While smaller than Seoul's expat scene, it is welcoming and active.

대전의 국제 커뮤니티는 KAIST와 유성구 연구기관을 중심으로 형성되어 있습니다. 정기적인 언어 교환, 국제 음식 모임, 주말 등산 모임이 활발합니다.

대전은 한국의 중심에서 여유롭고 저렴한 생활을 즐기면서, 주말마다 전국 어디든 쉽게 여행하고 싶은 강사에게 이상적인 도시입니다.

posts.title: Jeju 제주 — 한국의 하와이, 섬 생활의 매력
posts.body:
## Jeju Island — Korea's Tropical Paradise
## 제주도 — 한국의 하와이, 섬 생활의 매력

Jeju Island is South Korea's largest island, located off the southern coast. With a population of about 680,000 and a subtropical climate, it offers a dramatically different lifestyle from mainland Korea. Often called "Korea's Hawaii," Jeju is a UNESCO-designated natural wonder.

제주도는 대한민국 최대의 섬으로, 남해안에 위치하고 있습니다. 인구 약 68만 명, 아열대성 기후로 본토와는 확연히 다른 생활 환경을 제공합니다. "한국의 하와이"로 불리며, 유네스코 자연유산으로 지정되어 있습니다.

### Teaching in Jeju

Jeju has a smaller but growing ESL market. The island's popularity as a domestic tourism and education destination has driven demand for English teachers, particularly at hagwons and international schools in Jeju City and Seogwipo.

제주도의 ESL 시장은 규모는 작지만 성장 중입니다. 특히 제주시와 서귀포시의 학원과 국제학교에서 영어 강사 수요가 증가하고 있습니다.

- **Salary range**: 2,000,000 - 2,400,000 KRW/month
- **급여 범위**: 월 200만 ~ 240만 원
- **Key areas**: Jeju City (제주시) — more urban; Seogwipo (서귀포시) — coastal and scenic
- **주요 지역**: 제주시 (도시 중심), 서귀포시 (해안 경관)

### Island Life

**Climate**: Jeju is warmer than the mainland year-round. Winters are mild (rarely below 0 degrees C), and summers are warm and humid. Spring brings stunning canola flower fields, and autumn offers brilliant foliage.

**기후**: 제주도는 연중 본토보다 따뜻합니다. 겨울에도 영하로 잘 내려가지 않으며, 봄에는 유채꽃, 가을에는 단풍이 아름답습니다.

**Nature Everywhere**:
- **Hallasan Mountain** (한라산) — Korea's tallest peak at 1,950m, stunning hiking year-round
- **Olle Trails** (올레길) — 26 coastal walking routes circling the island (total 425 km)
- **Beaches**: Hyeopjae (협재), Jungmun (중문), Hamdeok (함덕) — crystal-clear water
- **Manjanggul Cave** (만장굴) — World-class lava tube, UNESCO Geopark
- **Seongsan Ilchulbong** (성산일출봉) — Iconic volcanic crater, sunrise viewpoint

### Cost of Living

| Item | Jeju | Seoul |
|------|------|-------|
| Studio rent | 300,000-500,000 KRW | 500,000-900,000 KRW |
| Meal (restaurant) | 8,000-12,000 KRW | 9,000-15,000 KRW |
| Groceries | Similar (some items pricier due to shipping) | Standard |
| A car or scooter | Highly recommended | Not needed |

**Important note**: Public transportation on Jeju is limited compared to mainland cities. Many teachers rent a car or scooter. Some schools provide transportation or are located near bus routes.

**참고**: 제주도의 대중교통은 본토 도시에 비해 제한적입니다. 많은 강사들이 자동차나 스쿠터를 이용합니다.

### Why Choose Jeju?

- Unparalleled natural beauty — ocean, mountains, forests, volcanic landscapes
- Slower pace of life compared to Seoul or Busan
- Unique culture and dialect (제주어)
- Fresh seafood: haenyeo (해녀) divers, black pork BBQ, Hallabong oranges
- Active outdoor lifestyle: surfing, diving, hiking, cycling
- Growing international community with cafes and guesthouses

### Considerations

- Limited nightlife and shopping compared to mainland cities
- Transportation requires planning (car recommended)
- Fewer international food options
- Occasional typhoons in late summer
- Flights to mainland are frequent and affordable (Seoul: ~1 hour, from 30,000 KRW)

제주도는 자연 속에서 여유로운 라이프스타일을 꿈꾸는 강사에게 특별한 경험을 선사합니다. BRIDGE가 제주 지역 포지션을 안내해 드립니다.

posts.title: Korean Food Guide 한국 음식 가이드 — 매일 먹게 될 음식들
posts.body:
## Korean Food Guide for ESL Teachers
## 한국 음식 가이드 — 매일 먹게 될 음식들

One of the greatest perks of living in Korea is the food. Whether you love spicy dishes or prefer mild flavors, Korean cuisine has something for everyone. Here is a practical guide to the meals you will encounter daily.

한국 생활의 가장 큰 장점 중 하나는 음식입니다. 매운 음식이든 순한 음식이든, 한국 요리에는 모든 입맛을 만족시킬 메뉴가 있습니다.

### Everyday Meals / 일상 식사

**Meals under 10,000 KRW (만 원 이하 한 끼)**

| Dish | Korean | Price Range | Spicy? |
|------|--------|-------------|--------|
| Kimchi Jjigae | 김치찌개 | 7,000-8,000 | Yes |
| Doenjang Jjigae | 된장찌개 | 7,000-8,000 | Mild |
| Bibimbap | 비빔밥 | 7,000-9,000 | Adjustable |
| Gimbap | 김밥 | 3,000-4,500 | No |
| Tteokbokki | 떡볶이 | 4,000-5,000 | Yes |
| Ramyeon | 라면 | 4,000-6,000 | Usually |
| Jajangmyeon | 자장면 | 6,000-8,000 | No |
| Kalguksu | 칼국수 | 7,000-9,000 | No |

**BBQ and Shared Meals (고기와 회식 메뉴)**

| Dish | Korean | Price (per person) | Spicy? |
|------|--------|-------------------|--------|
| Samgyeopsal (pork belly) | 삼겹살 | 12,000-16,000 | No |
| Galbi (beef ribs) | 갈비 | 18,000-30,000 | No |
| Dakgalbi (spicy chicken) | 닭갈비 | 11,000-14,000 | Yes |
| Budae Jjigae (army stew) | 부대찌개 | 10,000-13,000 | Yes |
| Gopchang (intestines) | 곱창 | 14,000-18,000 | Varies |

### Useful Korean Phrases for Ordering

- "여기요" (yeogiyo) — "Excuse me" (to call the server)
- "이거 주세요" (igeo juseyo) — "This one, please" (point at the menu)
- "덜 맵게 해주세요" (deol maepge haejuseyo) — "Less spicy, please"
- "안 맵게 해주세요" (an maepge haejuseyo) — "Not spicy, please"
- "물 주세요" (mul juseyo) — "Water, please"
- "계산이요" (gyesaniyo) — "Check, please"

### Dietary Restrictions

**Vegetarian/Vegan**: Korea is not the easiest country for strict vegetarians. Most soups and side dishes contain some form of meat or seafood stock. However, options are growing:

- Temple food restaurants (사찰음식) — fully vegetarian
- Say "고기 빼주세요" (gogi ppae juseyo) — "No meat, please"
- Seoul and larger cities have dedicated vegan restaurants
- Convenience store options: vegetable gimbap, plain rice triangles

채식주의자에게 한국은 쉽지 않을 수 있지만, 사찰음식 전문점이나 비건 레스토랑이 늘어나고 있습니다.

**Halal**: Halal options are available primarily in Seoul (Itaewon area) and near university districts. Apps like "Halal Korea" can help locate certified restaurants.

**Allergies**: Inform your server directly. "알레르기가 있어요" (allereugi-ga isseoyo) — "I have an allergy."

### Convenience Store Culture / 편의점 문화

Korean convenience stores (CU, GS25, 7-Eleven, Emart24) are on every corner and serve as budget-friendly meal spots:

- Triangle kimbap (삼각김밥): 1,000-1,500 KRW
- Cup ramyeon: 1,200-2,000 KRW (hot water provided)
- Lunchbox (도시락): 3,000-5,000 KRW
- Sandwiches: 2,000-3,500 KRW

### Delivery Culture / 배달 문화

Korea has one of the world's most advanced food delivery systems. Apps like **Baedal Minjok** (배달의민족), **Coupang Eats**, and **Yogiyo** (요기요) deliver virtually any restaurant food to your door, often within 30 minutes.

한국은 세계 최고 수준의 배달 문화를 가지고 있습니다. 배달의민족, 쿠팡이츠, 요기요 앱으로 거의 모든 음식을 30분 내에 배달받을 수 있습니다.

한국 음식은 다양하고 맛있으며, 대부분 매우 합리적인 가격입니다. 두려워하지 말고 새로운 음식에 도전해 보세요!

posts.title: Healthcare Guide 의료 가이드 — 병원 이용 및 건강보험 안내
posts.body:
## Healthcare & Hospital Guide for ESL Teachers in Korea
## 의료 가이드 — 병원 이용 및 건강보험 안내

South Korea has one of the best healthcare systems in the world — affordable, accessible, and high-quality. As a legally employed ESL teacher, you are covered by the National Health Insurance (NHI) system.

대한민국은 세계 최고 수준의 의료 시스템을 보유하고 있습니다. 합법적으로 고용된 ESL 강사는 국민건강보험의 적용을 받습니다.

### National Health Insurance (국민건강보험)

**Who is covered?** All E-2 visa holders employed at a registered workplace are enrolled in the National Health Insurance system. This is mandatory, not optional.

**가입 대상**: 등록된 사업장에 고용된 모든 E-2 비자 소지자는 국민건강보험에 의무 가입됩니다.

**How it works**:
- Your employer registers you when you begin work
- Premium is split 50/50 between you and your employer
- Monthly premium: approximately 70,000-100,000 KRW (your share)
- Covers about **70%** of medical costs at hospitals and clinics
- You pay the remaining 30% as a co-payment

**작동 방식**: 고용주가 가입 신청하며, 보험료는 사용자와 근로자가 50:50으로 부담합니다. 의료비의 약 70%를 보험이 부담합니다.

### Visiting a Hospital or Clinic / 병원 이용법

**Step 1. Choose the right facility**

| Facility | Korean | When to Use |
|----------|--------|-------------|
| Local clinic (의원) | 의원 / 내과 | Cold, flu, minor issues |
| General hospital (병원) | 병원 | Moderate issues, tests |
| University hospital (대학병원) | 대학병원 | Serious conditions, specialists |
| Emergency room (응급실) | 응급실 | Emergencies |
| Pharmacy (약국) | 약국 | Prescription pickup |

**Tip**: For everyday issues like a cold or stomach trouble, visit a local **내과** (naegwa — internal medicine clinic). No appointment needed. Walk in, take a number, and wait.

**팁**: 감기나 배탈 같은 일상적인 문제는 동네 내과에 가세요. 예약 없이 방문 가능합니다.

**Step 2. Bring your insurance card**

Present your **건강보험증** (Health Insurance Card) or your **Alien Registration Card** at reception. If you do not have your card yet, your ARC number is sufficient.

**Step 3. See the doctor**

Many doctors in Korea speak some English, especially at larger hospitals. If you are concerned about language:

- University hospitals often have **international clinics** with English-speaking staff
- The **1339 Health Helpline** provides medical consultation in English (call 1339)
- Translation apps work in a pinch

많은 의사가 기본적인 영어가 가능합니다. 대학병원에는 외국인 전용 클리닉이 있으며, 1339에 전화하면 영어 의료 상담을 받을 수 있습니다.

**Step 4. Get your prescription**

The doctor will print a prescription. Take it to any nearby **약국** (pharmacy). Pharmacies are everywhere — often right next to the hospital.

### Common Medical Costs (with insurance)

| Service | Approximate Co-pay |
|---------|-------------------|
| Clinic visit (cold/flu) | 5,000-10,000 KRW |
| Blood test | 10,000-20,000 KRW |
| Dental cleaning | 15,000-30,000 KRW |
| X-ray | 5,000-15,000 KRW |
| Prescription medication | 3,000-10,000 KRW |
| ER visit | 20,000-50,000 KRW |

### Dental and Eye Care / 치과 및 안과

- **Dental**: Basic procedures are covered by NHI. Cosmetic work (whitening, veneers) is not covered but still affordable by international standards
- **Eye care**: Eye exams and basic treatments are covered. Glasses and contacts are out-of-pocket but very affordable (glasses from 30,000 KRW)

치과: 기본 진료는 보험 적용. 안과: 검진 및 기본 치료 보험 적용. 안경은 자비 부담이나 매우 저렴합니다.

### Mental Health / 정신건강

Adjusting to life abroad can be stressful. Korea has mental health resources available:

- Counseling covered under NHI at psychiatric clinics (정신건강의학과)
- **Seoul Global Center**: Free counseling for foreigners in English
- **1393 Suicide Prevention Hotline**: Available 24/7 in Korean and English

해외 생활 적응이 어려울 수 있습니다. 정신건강의학과 상담은 보험 적용되며, 서울글로벌센터에서 무료 외국인 상담을 제공합니다.

### Emergency Numbers

- **119**: Fire and ambulance
- **1339**: Medical helpline (English available)
- **112**: Police
- **1345**: Immigration helpline

한국의 의료 시스템은 합리적이고 접근성이 높습니다. 아프면 참지 말고 가까운 병원을 방문하세요.

posts.title: Banking & Money Guide 금융 가이드 — 은행 계좌 및 송금 안내
posts.body:
## Banking & Money Guide for ESL Teachers
## 금융 가이드 — 은행 계좌 개설 및 송금 안내

Managing your finances in Korea is straightforward once you understand the system. This guide covers bank accounts, daily payments, and sending money home.

한국에서의 금융 생활은 시스템을 이해하면 매우 편리합니다. 이 가이드에서는 은행 계좌 개설, 일상 결제, 해외 송금을 안내합니다.

### Opening a Bank Account / 은행 계좌 개설

**When**: You can open an account after receiving your Alien Registration Card (ARC). Some banks allow opening with a passport, but features will be limited.

**시기**: 외국인등록증(ARC) 발급 후 개설 가능합니다. 일부 은행은 여권만으로도 개설 가능하지만 기능이 제한됩니다.

**What to bring**:
- Alien Registration Card (외국인등록증)
- Passport
- Phone number (Korean number required)
- Employment contract or school verification letter

**Popular banks for foreigners**:

| Bank | Korean | English App | Notes |
|------|--------|-------------|-------|
| KEB Hana Bank | KEB하나은행 | Yes | Best English support |
| Shinhan Bank | 신한은행 | Yes | Widely used |
| Woori Bank | 우리은행 | Yes | Large branch network |
| KB Kookmin Bank | KB국민은행 | Partial | Most branches nationwide |
| NH NongHyup | NH농협 | Limited | Common in rural areas |

**Recommendation**: **KEB Hana Bank** and **Shinhan Bank** have the best English-language support and foreigner-friendly services.

**추천**: KEB하나은행과 신한은행이 외국인 서비스가 가장 우수합니다.

### Daily Payments / 일상 결제

**Cards are king**: Korea is one of the most cashless societies in the world. Credit and debit cards are accepted virtually everywhere — even street food vendors.

**카드가 왕**: 한국은 세계에서 가장 카드 결제가 보편화된 나라 중 하나입니다. 길거리 음식점까지 카드 결제가 가능합니다.

**Mobile payments**: Once you have a Korean bank account, set up:
- **Samsung Pay** or **Apple Pay** (linked to Korean cards)
- **KakaoPay** — linked to KakaoTalk, used everywhere
- **Naver Pay** — popular for online shopping
- **T-money / Cashbee card** — for public transportation (recharge at convenience stores)

**모바일 결제**: 카카오페이, 네이버페이, 삼성페이 등을 설정하면 더욱 편리합니다. 교통카드(T-money)는 편의점에서 충전 가능합니다.

### Sending Money Home / 해외 송금

Most ESL teachers send a portion of their salary home each month. Here are the main options:

**Option 1: Wise (formerly TransferWise)** — Most Recommended
- Best exchange rates (mid-market rate)
- Low fees (usually 5,000-15,000 KRW per transfer)
- Transfer arrives in 1-2 business days
- Easy-to-use app in English
- Register with your Korean bank account

**Option 2: Bank Wire Transfer (은행 송금)**
- Available at any Korean bank
- Higher fees (20,000-30,000 KRW) and less favorable exchange rates
- Takes 2-5 business days
- Useful for very large transfers

**Option 3: SentBe / Remitly / OFX**
- Alternative fintech services with competitive rates
- Good backup options if Wise is unavailable

**Recommended strategy**: Open a **Wise** account and link your Korean bank. Set up monthly transfers for a consistent savings plan.

**추천 전략**: Wise 계정을 개설하고 한국 은행과 연결하세요. 매월 정기 송금을 설정하면 편리합니다.

### Tax & Pension / 세금 및 연금

**Income Tax**: Flat rate of **19%** for foreign workers (optional simplified method) or progressive rate (6-45%). Most ESL teachers benefit from the flat rate.

**National Pension (국민연금)**: You will contribute about 4.5% of your salary (employer matches 4.5%). **Important**: Citizens of countries with a pension agreement with Korea (USA, Canada, UK, Australia, etc.) can claim a **lump-sum refund** when leaving Korea.

**소득세**: 외국인 근로자 단일세율 19% 선택 가능. **국민연금**: 급여의 4.5% 부담 (사용자 4.5% 매칭). 한국과 연금 협정이 있는 국가(미국, 캐나다, 영국, 호주 등) 국민은 출국 시 일시금 반환 청구 가능.

### How to Claim Pension Refund / 연금 반환 청구

1. Visit the National Pension Service (국민연금공단) office before leaving Korea
2. Or apply online within 5 years of departure
3. Bring: ARC, passport, Korean bank account details (or foreign account)
4. Refund includes your contributions + employer contributions + interest

### Tips for Financial Success

- **Set a budget early**: Monthly expenses for a single teacher typically run 800,000-1,200,000 KRW (excluding rent if provided)
- **Save in KRW**: Exchange rates fluctuate — do not convert your entire salary every month
- **Use Kakao Bank or Toss**: Korean fintech apps with excellent features and no-fee transfers between Korean banks
- **Keep receipts**: You may need them for tax deductions

한국에서의 금융 관리는 체계적이고 편리합니다. 초기 설정만 잘 하면 매달 효율적으로 저축하고 송금할 수 있습니다.

posts.title: Transportation Guide — Getting Around Korea Like a Local
posts.body:
# Transportation in Korea — Complete Guide

Korea's public transport is world-class. Here's everything you need to know.

## T-money Card
- **Buy at any convenience store** (2,500 won for the card)
- **Recharge**: Convenience stores or subway station machines
- Works on: All buses, subways, and taxis nationwide
- **Transfer discount**: Free transfer between bus and subway within 30 minutes

## Subway
- Seoul Metro covers the entire capital region
- **Operating hours**: ~5:30 AM to ~12:00 AM
- Color-coded lines, English announcements, clear signage
- **Base fare**: 1,350 won (increases with distance)
- **Naver Map** gives real-time subway directions and estimated times

## Bus
- **Blue (간선)**: Long-distance trunk routes
- **Green (지선)**: Local neighborhood routes
- **Red (광역)**: Express between cities/suburbs
- **Yellow (순환)**: Circular routes within districts
- Tap T-money when boarding AND exiting

## KTX (Bullet Train)
- Seoul to Busan: 2h 30min (from 59,800 won)
- Seoul to Gwangju: 1h 50min
- Book on **SRT** or **Korail** app (English available)
- **Tip**: Book early for discounts; standing tickets are cheaper

## Taxi
- **Base fare**: ~4,800 won (varies by city)
- **Kakao T app**: Korea's Uber; shows fare estimate, English available
- **Late night** (12AM-4AM): 20-40% surcharge
- **International taxis**: Available at airports, English-speaking drivers

## Intercity Bus
- Cheaper than KTX, covers more destinations
- Book on **Express Bus** app or pay at the terminal
- Most buses have USB charging and reclining seats

## Driving
- International Driving Permit valid for 1 year
- **Not recommended** — parking is expensive, traffic is heavy, public transport is better
- If needed: Korean license conversion is straightforward with your IDP

posts.title: Phone & Internet Setup — Your Digital Life in Korea
posts.body:
# Setting Up Your Phone & Internet in Korea

Korea has the fastest internet in the world. Here's how to get connected.

## Mobile Phone Plans
### Prepaid SIM (first option)
- Buy at airport arrival hall (KT, SKT, LG U+)
- **30-day tourist SIM**: ~35,000-55,000 won (unlimited data)
- Only need passport

### Monthly Plan (after ARC)
- Visit any carrier store with passport + ARC
- **Budget plans**: 25,000-35,000 won/month (enough data)
- **Unlimited plans**: 45,000-65,000 won/month
- **MVNOs** (cheaper): KT M Mobile, SK 7mobile — same network, lower prices

## Essential Apps
| App | Purpose |
|-----|---------|
| KakaoTalk | Messaging (everyone uses it) |
| Naver Map | Navigation (Google Maps doesn't work well) |
| Papago | Translation (better than Google for Korean) |
| Coupang | Online shopping (next-day delivery) |
| Toss | Banking, payments, splitting bills |
| 배달의민족 | Food delivery |
| Kakao T | Taxi, parking, bike rental |
| AirVisual | Air quality check |
| Subway Korea | Subway navigation |

## Home Internet
- Usually **included in your apartment** or set up by school
- If you need to set up: KT, SK, LG U+ — 30,000-40,000 won/month
- Speed: 100Mbps to 1Gbps (yes, really)
- Installation: Same-day or next-day in most areas

## WiFi
- **Free WiFi everywhere**: Subway, buses, cafes, convenience stores
- Quality varies — have your mobile data as backup
- **Public WiFi names**: iptime, KT_GiGA, U+zone, olleh WiFi

## Banking Apps
- Your Korean bank will have an app with English support
- **Toss**: Modern fintech app, great for daily use
- **Kakao Pay**: Linked to KakaoTalk, widely accepted
- International transfers: Use **Wise** for best rates

posts.title: Welcome to BRIDGE — How It Works
posts.body:
## Welcome to BRIDGE Job

Thank you for choosing **BRIDGE** as your partner in finding ESL teaching positions in South Korea. We are a recruitment platform dedicated to connecting qualified English teachers with reputable schools across the country.

### How BRIDGE Works

1. **Browse Jobs** -- Visit our Jobs page to explore current openings. You can filter by region, school type, salary range, and start date.
2. **Create Your Profile** -- Submit your basic information and upload key documents. Your data is encrypted and handled securely.
3. **Apply Directly** -- Found a position you like? Click Apply and fill out the application form. Your profile details are sent directly to the hiring school.
4. **Interview & Offer** -- Schools review applications and schedule interviews (usually via video call). If selected, you will receive a formal offer letter.
5. **Visa & Travel** -- Once you accept an offer, BRIDGE and your school will guide you through the E-2 visa process, document preparation, and travel arrangements.

### What Makes BRIDGE Different

- **No fees for teachers.** Our service is completely free for job seekers. Schools pay the placement fee.
- **Verified employers.** Every school listed on our platform has been reviewed for legitimacy and compliance with Korean labor law.
- **Ongoing support.** We do not disappear after placement. Our team is available throughout your contract for questions, concerns, or disputes.
- **Privacy first.** Sensitive personal data (passport info, background checks, medical records) is encrypted at rest using AES-256 encryption.

### Getting Started

Browse the other posts in this section for detailed guides on documents, contracts, housing, insurance, and more. If you have any questions, use the Inquiry form on our website or email us at support@bridgejob.co.kr.

We look forward to helping you start your teaching journey in Korea!

posts.title: Application Process Step by Step
posts.body:
## Application Process Step by Step

Applying for an ESL teaching position in Korea through BRIDGE is straightforward. Here is exactly what to expect at each stage.

### Step 1: Browse & Select

Visit the Jobs page and use the filters to narrow down positions that match your preferences. Pay attention to:

- **Location** (Seoul, Busan, Daegu, rural areas, etc.)
- **School type** (public school via EPIK/POE, hagwon, international school)
- **Start date** and contract length
- **Salary and benefits** (housing, airfare, severance)

### Step 2: Prepare Your Documents

Before applying, make sure you have digital copies of your key documents ready. See the Required Documents Checklist post for the full list. At minimum, you will need:

- Resume/CV
- Recent photo
- Copy of your degree
- Copy of your passport

### Step 3: Submit Your Application

Click the Apply button on any job listing. Fill in the application form completely. Incomplete applications are often rejected by schools without review.

### Step 4: Interview

If a school is interested, they will contact you (usually within 1-2 weeks) to schedule a video interview. Tips for success:

- Dress professionally, even for video calls
- Prepare a short self-introduction
- Have questions ready about the school, curriculum, and daily schedule
- Test your camera and microphone beforehand

### Step 5: Offer & Contract Review

If the interview goes well, the school will send a contract. **Read every clause carefully.** Refer to our Understanding Your Korean Contract post for guidance. Ask BRIDGE if anything is unclear.

### Step 6: Visa Processing

Once the contract is signed, your school will issue a Visa Issuance Number (VIN). Take this to your nearest Korean consulate to obtain your E-2 visa.

### Step 7: Arrival & Orientation

Your school or BRIDGE will arrange airport pickup and help you settle in. Some public school programs include a multi-day orientation.

### Timeline

From application to arrival, the entire process typically takes **6 to 12 weeks**. Start early to allow time for document apostilles and visa processing.

posts.title: Required Documents Checklist
posts.body:
## Required Documents Checklist

To teach English in South Korea on an E-2 visa, you must prepare several documents. Processing times vary, so start gathering these **as early as possible** -- ideally 8-12 weeks before your intended start date.

### Mandatory Documents

- **Valid Passport** -- Must be valid for at least 12 months beyond your intended entry date.
- **Bachelor's Degree (Original)** -- Any major is accepted. The degree must be from an accredited university in a country where English is the official language.
- **Apostilled Degree** -- Your degree must be apostilled by the appropriate authority in your home country (e.g., U.S. Department of State, UK FCDO).
- **National-Level Criminal Background Check** -- FBI check (USA), ACRO (UK), RCMP (Canada), AFP (Australia). Must be recent (within 6 months).
- **Apostilled Criminal Background Check** -- Same apostille process as the degree.
- **Sealed University Transcripts** -- Some schools and immigration offices require these.
- **Health Check** -- Completed after arrival in Korea at a designated hospital. Costs approximately 90,000 KRW.
- **Passport-Size Photos** -- Bring at least 5-6 copies (3.5cm x 4.5cm, white background).

### Recommended but Optional

- **TEFL/TESOL/CELTA Certificate** -- Not legally required for the E-2 visa, but many schools prefer or require 120+ hours. An accredited certificate can significantly improve your job prospects and salary.
- **Teaching License** -- Required for some public school positions, especially international schools.
- **Letters of Recommendation** -- From previous employers or professors. Helpful but not always required.
- **Experience Certificates** -- If you have prior teaching experience, bring signed letters from former schools.

### Important Notes

- All apostilled documents must be **originals**, not photocopies.
- Some countries process apostilles in 2-3 weeks; others can take 8+ weeks. Plan accordingly.
- Digital scans of all documents should be kept as backups.
- BRIDGE will never ask you to email original documents. Upload scanned copies through our secure portal only.

If you are unsure about the apostille process for your country, contact us and we will guide you through it.

posts.title: Understanding Your Korean Contract
posts.body:
## Understanding Your Korean Contract

Your employment contract is the most important document in your teaching experience. Korean labor law provides strong protections for foreign workers, but only if your contract is clear and compliant.

### Key Clauses to Review

**Contract Duration**
- Standard contracts are 12 months. Some schools offer 6-month contracts, but these are less common and may offer fewer benefits.

**Salary**
- Monthly salary should be stated in Korean Won (KRW). Typical range: 2.1M - 2.8M KRW/month for entry-level positions.
- Salary must be paid on a fixed date each month (commonly the 10th or 25th).

**Working Hours**
- Teaching hours (actual classroom time) vs. working hours (total time at school) are different. Ensure both are defined.
- Legal maximum: 40 working hours per week. Overtime must be compensated.

**Housing**
- Most contracts include **free furnished housing** (single studio apartment) or a housing allowance (typically 300,000-500,000 KRW/month).
- Clarify who pays utilities, internet, and maintenance.

**Airfare**
- Round-trip airfare is standard. Some schools only cover one-way. Confirm which.

**Severance Pay**
- After completing a full 12-month contract, you are entitled to **one month's salary** as severance. This is Korean law, not a bonus.

**Vacation & Sick Leave**
- Public schools: typically 18-20 paid vacation days plus national holidays.
- Hagwons: typically 10 days paid vacation. Confirm whether you choose the dates or the school assigns them.

### Red Flags

Watch out for these warning signs:

- No mention of severance pay
- "Salary will be discussed later"
- Penalty clauses for early termination that exceed one month's salary
- Requirements to surrender your passport or alien registration card
- No clear working hours or excessive overtime expectations

### What to Do

1. Read the entire contract before signing.
2. Ask questions -- a good employer welcomes them.
3. If anything seems wrong, send the contract to BRIDGE for a free review.
4. Keep a signed copy for your own records at all times.

posts.title: Salary Guide for ESL Teachers in Korea
posts.body:
## Salary Guide for ESL Teachers in Korea

Salaries for ESL teachers in South Korea depend on your qualifications, experience, school type, and location. Below is a general guide based on current market rates.

### Typical Monthly Salaries (KRW)

| School Type | Entry Level | Experienced (2+ yrs) |
|---|---|---|
| Public School (EPIK) | 2,000,000 - 2,300,000 | 2,300,000 - 2,700,000 |
| Hagwon (Private Academy) | 2,100,000 - 2,500,000 | 2,500,000 - 3,000,000 |
| International School | 2,500,000 - 3,500,000 | 3,500,000 - 5,000,000+ |
| University | 2,300,000 - 3,000,000 | 3,000,000 - 3,800,000 |

### Factors That Increase Your Salary

- **TEFL/TESOL/CELTA certification** -- 100,000 to 200,000 KRW/month bump at many schools.
- **Teaching license** from your home country.
- **Master's degree** -- especially relevant for university positions.
- **Prior Korea experience** -- returning teachers typically negotiate higher salaries.
- **Location** -- rural or less popular cities may offer higher pay to attract candidates.

### Deductions

Your gross salary will have the following deductions:

- **Income Tax**: approximately 3-5% for most teachers
- **National Pension**: 4.5% (your employer matches this; may be refundable when you leave Korea depending on your nationality)
- **National Health Insurance**: approximately 3.5% (employer pays the other half)
- **Resident Tax**: 0.3-0.5%

Net take-home is typically **88-92%** of gross salary.

### Saving Potential

With free housing provided, many teachers save **1,000,000 - 1,500,000 KRW per month** (approximately $750-$1,100 USD). Teachers in rural areas or those who are frugal can save even more.

### Payment

Salary is deposited into a Korean bank account, which you will open shortly after arrival. Most teachers also set up international wire transfers or use services like Wise (formerly TransferWise) to send money home.

For specific salary questions about a listing on BRIDGE, contact us and we can provide additional context.

posts.title: Housing Arrangements & Key Money
posts.body:
## Housing Arrangements & Key Money

One of the biggest benefits of teaching in Korea is that most employers provide **free furnished housing**. Here is what you need to know.

### Employer-Provided Housing

Most teaching contracts include a single-occupancy studio apartment (called a "one-room" or "weonrum" in Korean). Typically included:

- Bed, desk, chair, wardrobe
- Washing machine
- Refrigerator, stove or cooktop
- Air conditioning / heating unit
- Basic kitchen supplies (varies)

**Not typically included:** towels, bedding, toiletries, dishes, cookware. Budget 100,000-200,000 KRW for initial household supplies.

### Housing Allowance

Some schools offer a monthly housing allowance instead of an apartment (usually 300,000-500,000 KRW). If you take this option:

- You will need to find your own apartment.
- You may need to pay **key money** (see below).
- You gain more freedom in choosing location and apartment quality.

### Understanding Key Money (Jeonse and Wolse)

Korea has a unique rental system:

- **Jeonse (Key Money Deposit):** A large lump-sum deposit (often tens of millions of KRW) with no monthly rent. The landlord invests the deposit and returns it at the end of the lease. This system is less common for foreigners on short-term contracts.
- **Wolse (Monthly Rent):** A smaller deposit (typically 1-5 million KRW) plus monthly rent. This is what most ESL teachers use if finding their own housing.

If your employer provides housing, they handle the deposit. If you are finding your own place, confirm with your school whether they will cover or advance the deposit.

### Utilities

Monthly utility costs (electricity, gas, water, internet) typically total **80,000-150,000 KRW** depending on the season. Summer air conditioning and winter heating are the biggest expenses.

### Tips

- Inspect your apartment on arrival and document any existing damage with photos.
- Keep all receipts for repairs or issues.
- Confirm in your contract who pays for maintenance and repairs.
- If your apartment has problems (mold, broken appliances, pests), report them to your school immediately.

BRIDGE can help mediate any housing disputes between you and your employer.

posts.title: Health Insurance in Korea
posts.body:
## Health Insurance in Korea

South Korea has an excellent national health insurance system, and as a legal foreign worker, you are **required** to enroll.

### National Health Insurance (NHI)

- Managed by the **National Health Insurance Service (NHIS)**.
- Enrollment is mandatory for all E-2 visa holders employed for more than one month.
- Your employer handles the registration process.
- Coverage begins from your enrollment date.

### Cost

- The premium is approximately **7% of your salary**, split 50/50 between you and your employer.
- For a teacher earning 2,300,000 KRW/month, your share is roughly 80,000 KRW/month.
- The premium is automatically deducted from your paycheck.

### What Is Covered

- Doctor visits (general practice and specialists)
- Hospital stays
- Prescription medication
- Dental care (basic procedures)
- Mental health services
- Traditional Korean medicine (hanbang)

You typically pay a **copay of 20-40%** depending on the type of service and facility. For example:

- General clinic visit: 5,000 - 15,000 KRW out of pocket
- Specialist visit: 10,000 - 30,000 KRW out of pocket
- Prescription drugs: 2,000 - 10,000 KRW

### What Is Not Covered

- Cosmetic procedures
- Most dental work beyond basic care (crowns, orthodontics)
- Some advanced imaging (full-body MRI, etc.)
- Procedures deemed not medically necessary

### Mandatory Health Check

Within the first few weeks of arrival, you must complete a **health examination** at a designated hospital. This includes:

- Blood test (HIV, syphilis, hepatitis)
- Urinalysis (drug screening)
- Chest X-ray (tuberculosis)
- Physical exam

Cost: approximately 90,000 KRW. Some employers reimburse this; confirm with your school.

### Tips

- Carry your insurance card at all times.
- Many doctors in Seoul and major cities speak English. In smaller cities, bring a Korean-speaking friend or use a translation app.
- For emergencies, dial **119** (fire/ambulance) or go to the nearest hospital emergency room.
- Keep receipts for all medical expenses for your records and potential tax deductions.

posts.title: Pension & Tax Information
posts.body:
## Pension & Tax Information

Understanding your tax and pension obligations in Korea will help you plan your finances and avoid surprises.

### National Pension (NPS)

- **Contribution rate:** 9% of your gross salary, split equally (4.5% from you, 4.5% from your employer).
- Enrollment is **mandatory** for most E-2 visa holders.

**Lump-Sum Refund:** Citizens of countries **without** a social security agreement with Korea (including the USA, Canada, and most others) can claim a **lump-sum refund** of their contributions when they permanently leave Korea. You apply at the National Pension Service office before departure or within 5 years of leaving.

**Exceptions:** Citizens of countries **with** a social security totalization agreement (including the UK and Australia, among others) may have different rules. Check with the NPS or your embassy.

### Income Tax

Korea uses a progressive income tax system, but most ESL teachers fall into the lower brackets:

- **Flat rate option:** Foreign workers can elect a **19% flat tax** on gross income (no deductions) instead of the standard progressive rates. For most ESL teachers, the standard rate is lower.
- **Standard progressive rate:** The first 14 million KRW of annual income is taxed at 6%, and 14M-50M at 15%. With deductions, most teachers pay an effective rate of **3-5%**.
- **Resident tax:** An additional 10% of your income tax amount (not your salary).

### Year-End Tax Settlement

In January/February, your employer will conduct a **year-end tax settlement** ("yeonmal jeonsan"). This is similar to filing taxes. You may receive a small refund or owe a small additional amount.

### What You Need to Do

1. Obtain your **Alien Registration Card (ARC)** -- required for opening a bank account and tax purposes.
2. Your employer handles tax withholding from each paycheck.
3. Keep records of any additional deductions you may be eligible for (medical expenses, donations, etc.).
4. Before leaving Korea, visit the NPS office to apply for your pension refund.
5. Request a **Certificate of Tax Payment** from your district tax office if needed for your home country tax filing.

### Important

Some countries (like the USA) require citizens to **report worldwide income**, even if taxes are paid in Korea. Consult a tax professional in your home country about the Foreign Earned Income Exclusion or applicable tax treaties.

posts.title: Airport Pickup & Settlement Guide
posts.body:
## Airport Pickup & Settlement Guide

Arriving in a new country can be overwhelming. Here is a step-by-step guide for your first days in Korea.

### Before You Fly

- Confirm your flight details with your school at least one week in advance.
- Pack essentials in your carry-on: passport, contract copies, school contact info, some Korean Won (200,000-300,000 KRW recommended), and any medications you need.
- Download useful apps: **KakaoTalk** (Korea's main messenger -- your school will likely contact you here), **Naver Map** or **KakaoMap**, **Papago** (translation).

### Airport Arrival (Incheon International Airport)

1. **Immigration:** Present your passport with E-2 visa. The officer may ask about your employer and contract length.
2. **Baggage claim:** Collect your luggage.
3. **Customs:** If you have nothing to declare, use the green lane.
4. **Meeting point:** Your school representative or BRIDGE coordinator will meet you in the arrivals hall. Look for a sign with your name.

If no one is there, do not panic. Contact your school via KakaoTalk or call the emergency number provided in your welcome packet.

### First Week Checklist

- **Alien Registration Card (ARC):** Your employer should take you to the local Immigration Office within 90 days, but ideally within the first 2 weeks. Bring your passport, contract, passport photos, and the application fee (30,000 KRW). Processing takes 2-3 weeks.
- **Bank Account:** Open a Korean bank account (Hana, Shinhan, KB Kookmin, or Woori are foreigner-friendly). You need your passport and ARC (some banks allow opening with just a passport, but functionality will be limited).
- **Phone:** Get a Korean phone number. You can get a prepaid SIM at the airport or sign up for a plan once you have your ARC. Budget 40,000-60,000 KRW/month for a standard plan.
- **Health Check:** Schedule your mandatory medical exam (see Health Insurance post).
- **Explore Your Neighborhood:** Find the nearest convenience store, grocery store, subway station, and hospital.

### Transportation

- Get a **T-money card** (available at convenience stores) for buses and subway. Tap to pay; auto-recharge options available.
- Buses and subways run from approximately 5:30 AM to midnight.
- Taxis are affordable. Base fare is approximately 4,800 KRW. Use the KakaoTaxi app for easy hailing.

### Your First Days at School

- Arrive early and dress professionally.
- Bring your documents (passport, degree, ARC receipt).
